title: playwright newContext with ignoreHTTPSErrors: true — the browser context accepts any TLS certificate (MITM)

params.passDetail: browser.newContext validates TLS certificates (ignoreHTTPSErrors is not true) — the context rejects forged or invalid certificates.
params.failDetail: browser.newContext is called with ignoreHTTPSErrors: true, so every page in this Playwright context accepts ANY TLS certificate. In a server-side scraper, SSR renderer, or automated flow that handles authenticated content, a network attacker can present a forged certificate and man-in-the-middle the session — injecting content or capturing credentials. Remove ignoreHTTPSErrors: true; trust a private CA explicitly if one is required.
params.absentCallDetail: No newContext() call in this scope; this rule does not apply here.
params.absentArgDetail: browser.newContext does not set ignoreHTTPSErrors; the secure default (validate) applies.

provenance.note: ignoreHTTPSErrors: true is copied from test setups (where a self-signed dev cert is expected) into server-side automation that touches real, authenticated sites — silently disabling MITM protection in production.
